alternatives.name: Anytype
alternatives.benefits: Local-first data model, highly modular and decentralized
alternatives.notes: Great for idea development, second brain tasks, no external dependencies
alternatives.recommendation: Primary fit for offline-first, second-brain backbone syncing with modular architecture

alternatives.name: Reflect Notes
alternatives.benefits: Ultra-fast graph linking between ideas; great for project-memory overlays
alternatives.recommendation: Secondary fit for deep graph-memory, perfect to extend cursor-memory-bank work

alternatives.name: Tana
alternatives.benefits: Customizable structure + actions/events, fits multi-layer agent flows
alternatives.recommendation: Experimental fit for dynamic agent behavior or cross-task automation

alternatives.name: Logseq
alternatives.benefits: Markdown local-first, backlinks; amazing for living system maps
alternatives.recommendation: Secondary fit for deep graph-memory, perfect to extend cursor-memory-bank work

alternatives.name: Capacities
alternatives.benefits: Pages are types of objects (tasks, ideas, bookmarks)
alternatives.recommendation: Experimental fit for idea growth & prototyping

alternatives.name: Supernotes
alternatives.benefits: Highly modular, atomic units of knowledge—perfect for minimalism + rapid linking
alternatives.recommendation: Experimental fit for idea growth & prototyping

actions: Create Atomic Note (one idea per note)
Tag to Phase (Discovery, Research, Planning, Building, Scaling)
Draft rough diagram (mindmap, system map, workflow)
steps.principle: Visualize systems. Keep flexible and lightweight.
Only promote mature ideas. Protect system memory.
Structure only what deserves to be structured.

workflow.motto: Capture lightly, connect deeply, commit only when clarity demands.

capture: Atomic First: Break ideas into the smallest meaningful units.
Phase Tag Early: Always tag notes immediately (Discovery, Research, Planning, Building, Scaling).
Link Boldly: Build webs of related knowledge — ideas should connect or be challenged.
visualization: Draft Fast, Think Clear: Focus on conveying logical flow, not polished aesthetics.
Save in Both Formats: .excalidraw (source) always; .svg optionally for clean visuals.
Label, Not Explain: Diagrams should largely explain themselves via labels and flows.
validation: Checklist Discipline: Never skip the Flow Gate — check Confidence, Atomicity, Logical Links, Naming.
Delay Promotion: If uncertain, keep working inside Anytype/Excalidraw. Promote only when coherence crystallizes.
promotion: Slugify Everything: Use clean, lowercased, hyphenated file names (phase2-user-flow-map.excalidraw).
Folder Respect: Diagrams in /diagrams/, notes in /cursor-memory-bank/ — maintain clear separation.
Commit With Reason: Every commit into Bolt DIY memory must have a "why now" justification.

anytype.anchorTemplate:
# 🧭 Reminder Motto
> **"Capture lightly, connect deeply, commit only when clarity demands."**

---

# 🧩 Phase Structure
- 🏁 **Discovery Phase** — Raw ideas, seeds, inspiration
- 🔍 **Research Phase** — Linked knowledge webs, technical exploration
- 🛠 **Planning Phase** — Structured drafts, early systems modeling
- 🏗 **Building Phase** — Concrete design, architecture, flows
- 🚀 **Scaling Phase** — Optimization, scaling systems, advanced integration

---

# 🛡 Flow Reminder
[Anytype (Idea Capture)] → [Excalidraw (Visual Draft)] → [Flow Gate Validation] → [Bolt DIY Commit]
